entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course uses the book God's Big Picture to give students a zoomed-out view of Bible history and show the outline of God's Kingdom plan from beginning to end.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grade 9

rows.label: Bible History: Grade 9
rows.books: God’s Big Picture: Tracing the Storyline of the Bible

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Bible History: Grade 9
Check the Extra Helpings for grades 9-12 resources for devotional reading, Sunday reading, seasonal reading, and more.

books.title: God’s Big Picture: Tracing the Storyline of the Bible

blocks.text: Take a moment to recall what you read previously in the same book or topic.

Read the passage assigned or read for the number of minutes allocated. If a book is challenging for you, try stopping to narrate to yourself silently now and then to make sure you are tracking. Or you could listen to an audiobook version of the text and read along. You may also want to read aloud to yourself. If a book is an easy read, enjoy the ride! Keep an eye out for quotes to put in your Commonplace Book or events or characters to put down in your Book of Centuries.

Process and integrate your reading by narrating either orally or in writing. Some lesson plans will give you a suggestion of how best to narrate. You might want to set a timer to keep yourself on track, so you stop reading with enough time to narrate.

Read notes in your Study Bible or commentary. Look up places mentioned in the text in your Bible atlas. Add something to your Book of Centuries or Commonplace Book. Jot down additional reflections and thoughts in your Bible notebook or share with others.

Read over the lesson plans to familiarize yourself with the content for accountability and interest. If doing the lessons together, use a picture, a map, or an engaging question to rouse students' interest before reading. You may also highlight one or two unfamiliar terms if needed. If in a classroom context, you may choose to leave larger doctrinal conversations for students to have with their parents at home.

lessons.title: 30m Bible History: Grade 9 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture

➜ INTRO
You will be reading God's Big Picture slowly over the entire year. Take a minute to acquaint yourself with the structure of the book. Notice the charts included in the text; don't skip these as you read the assigned selections—they are important supports. You may wish to read through the Bible study section at the end of each chapter but they are not generally assigned.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
God's Big Picture p.9-16 
Preface and Introduction up to middle of p.16

lessons.title: 30m Bible History: Grade 9 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture

➜ READ
p.16-23, continuing Introduction

➜ WRITE
Reflect on Fig.5 (p.22); consider how this applied to your Old Testament readings last year. Keep it in mind as you read this year's selections, perhaps using it as the basis for some of your Bible journal writings during other Bible lessons.

➜ READ
Use any extra lesson time today to finish other Bible lessons or do some reading from books suggested in Extra Helpings.

lessons.title: 30m Bible History: Grade 9 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture, "Four Views of Revelation"

➜ INTRO
Chapter 8, "The Perfected Kingdom", covers topics from Revelation. Theologians have interpreted the timeline of events differently throughout church history. The four main views are mentioned briefly in this text. An article by Dr. Patrick Zukeran is provided for a bit more context. Please note that though this is a fairly balanced overview, he does tend toward the futurist view.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.147-150

➜ READ & DISCUSS
∞ Article Link: "Four Views of Revelation"
For a bit more information, read Zukeran's article.

Exam Week 

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
God's Big Picture

terms.content:
· God's Big Picture: Choose one: 
a) What does the author mean by “God's people, God's place, and God's rule and blessing”?
b) List the Old Testament covenants and explain how God's grace is shown through them AND Tell what you know about the "new covenant"
c) Create The Kingdom of God Chart for “The pattern of the kingdom, The perished kingdom, and The promised kingdom.”

· God's Big Picture: How is "God's promise of the kingdom partially fulfilled in the history of Israel"?
OR
Discuss Figure 28 (p.110), "The End of the Old Testament" telling what you know of God's people, God's place, and God's rule and blessing as it relates to the time periods depicted.
· God's Big Picture: If you had to answer someone's question about what the Bible was all about, how would you do it using the framework of God's Big Picture as your model?
